claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 16 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake

Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and any children you have.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK. I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. I have two children - Alex who is 16, and Blake who is 5.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have two children. I'll need to assess each of them separately as Child Benefit eligibility is determined on a per-child basis.

Let me start by gathering some details about both children.

**For Blake (age 5): Are you responsible for Blake by living with them, or are you making financial contributions towards their upkeep such as food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Blake doesn't actually live with me. Their other parent has them living with them, and they're the one who looks after Blake day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Blake lives with their other parent who provides day-to-day care.

**Are you making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep, such as payments for food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards their accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask about Alex, who is 16.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. So Alex also doesn't live with you.

**Are you making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, such as payments for food, clothes, pocket money, or contributions towards their accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Alex's upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, food, and other essentials.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now, since Alex is 16 years old, I need to understand their current situation better.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training? For example, are they in school, college, university, or an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education - doing A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. Now I need to clarify the financial contributions you're making towards Alex.

**Are your financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (clothes, food, essentials) equal to or greater than the Child Benefit payment amount?** 

For context, the Child Benefit payment is currently £23.80 per week for the first child and £15.80 per week for subsequent children. So I'm asking whether your weekly contributions are at least equivalent to these amounts.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute enough to cover that. I give £50 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for that clarity. I have one more question to complete my assessment.

**For Blake (age 5), who lives with their other parent - is only one person claiming Child Benefit for Blake, or could there be multiple claims?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have all the details I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both children.

Let me explain my findings:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex, but not for Blake.**

**Alex (age 16):** ✅ **Eligible**
- You're making sufficient financial contributions (£50 per week) towards their upkeep
- Alex is still in approved education (A-levels), which allows you to continue claiming even though they're over 16
- This means you meet the criteria for being responsible for Alex

**Blake (age 5):** ❌ **Not Eligible**
- Blake doesn't live with you
- You're not making any financial contributions towards their upkeep
- Their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them, and only one person can claim per child

If you'd like to proceed with a claim for Alex, you can make a claim through the Child Benefit system.

reasonings: Blake is not eligible for Child Benefit under your claim because: (1) Blake does not live with you, and (2) you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. According to the rules, you must either live with a child or pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit (or in kind) towards their upkeep to be responsible for them. Additionally, Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim for each child.

Alex is eligible for Child Benefit under your claim because: (1) Although Alex does not live with you, you are making financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep of £50 per week, which exceeds the Child Benefit payment threshold. (2) Alex is 16 years old but still qualifies because they are in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). (3) Your contributions towards Alex's maintenance meet the requirement of being at least equivalent to the Child Benefit payment amount.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex only. Alex qualifies as you are making sufficient financial contributions (£50 per week) towards their upkeep while they remain in approved education. You are not eligible for Blake as you are not living with them, making no financial contributions, and their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Blake is not eligible for Child Benefit under your claim because: (1) Blake does not live with you, and (2) you are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. According to the rules, you must either live with a child or pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit (or in kind) towards their upkeep to be responsible for them. Additionally, Blake's other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and only one person can claim for each child.

Alex is eligible for Child Benefit under your claim because: (1) Although Alex does not live with you, you are making financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep of £50 per week, which exceeds the Child Benefit payment threshold. (2) Alex is 16 years old but still qualifies because they are in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). (3) Your contributions towards Alex's maintenance meet the requirement of being at least equivalent to the Child Benefit payment amount.

response.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex only. Alex qualifies as you are making sufficient financial contributions (£50 per week) towards their upkeep while they remain in approved education. You are not eligible for Blake as you are not living with them, making no financial contributions, and their other parent is already claiming Child Benefit for them. Only one person can claim Child Benefit per child.
